_comment: Source-controlled firmware release notes shown read-only in the dashboard. Edit this file in the repo when you push a new firmware build — do NOT edit it through the web UI. The newest release of each model is the build the server currently serves over OTA. Keyed by the model string the device reports during pairing.

releases.title: Add reTerminal E1001 support, custom-device system, auto-changelog & PhotoDock rename
changes: Multi-board firmware:
Board-abstraction (board.h) + second PlatformIO env; one codebase builds the
Waveshare PhotoPainter (E6) and the Seeed reTerminal E1001 (UC8179 7.5" mono).
UC8179 driver + B/W Floyd-Steinberg dithering (4-gray is phase 2); ADC battery
(no AXP2101); 3 buttons; reports its own model id.
Model-aware build/OTA: each board's binary lives in firmware_build/<model>/ and
devices only ever pull their own firmware.
Custom devices (admin-only): register a GitHub firmware repo; the server clones
and builds it (streamed log) into a flashable + OTA-able device type. Wizard
device-type dropdown and OTA model resolution are now dynamic.
Auto-changelog: copy_firmware.py records a firmware-changelog entry from the git
commit on each build (once per commit, stamped with the build hash), so the
changelog never goes stale; the server marks the running build by hash.
Branding: rename stale "Photo Display"/"PhotoDisplay"/"PhotoPainter" product
strings to PhotoDock (Wi-Fi AP is now PhotoDock-XXXX); kept the factual Waveshare
hardware name and model id.

releases.title: Initial reTerminal E1001 support
changes: New board target for the Seeed reTerminal E1001 — 7.5" UC8179 e-paper, ESP32-S3.
Phase 1 brings the panel up in black & white (Floyd–Steinberg dithered photos) to validate the hardware; 4-level grayscale is next.
Battery read via the on-board ADC divider; the SY6974B charger runs autonomously.
Shares the rest of the platform: WiFi pairing, OTA, sleep schedule, fit modes and the center button for next-photo / sleep.

releases.title: Add reTerminal E1001 support, custom-device system, auto-changelog & PhotoDock rename
changes: Multi-board firmware:
Board-abstraction (board.h) + second PlatformIO env; one codebase builds the
Waveshare PhotoPainter (E6) and the Seeed reTerminal E1001 (UC8179 7.5" mono).
UC8179 driver + B/W Floyd-Steinberg dithering (4-gray is phase 2); ADC battery
(no AXP2101); 3 buttons; reports its own model id.
Model-aware build/OTA: each board's binary lives in firmware_build/<model>/ and

Custom devices (admin-only): register a GitHub firmware repo; the server clones

hardware name and model id.

releases.title: Add battery, sleep schedule, fit modes, usage dashboard & offline detection
changes: Firmware: keep panel rails powered across sleep for reliable wake refresh;
enable AXP2101 LiPo charging + fuel gauge with charge/USB reporting; per-screen
sleep schedule with minute/hour-aligned wakes; radio-off during refresh and
cached-WiFi wakes for battery; OTA 'updating'/'update complete' screens.
Backend: per-screen and per-photo fit mode (contain/cover); sleep-window logic;
interval-based offline detection with a sleeping state; system metrics endpoint;
read-only per-model firmware changelog.
Frontend: settings split into Usage/Logs/Users/Server tabs with CPU/RAM/storage
gauges; charging indicator; sleep + fit controls; removed redundant global
brightness/saturation (now per-screen).

releases.title: KEY button advances to the next photo
changes: Fix the KEY button not skipping to the next photo — a quick tap was being thrown away as electrical noise because the button was only checked after the battery readout, by which point the tap had already been released. The button wake now counts as a short press on its own, so a tap reliably advances the photo.
Hold the KEY button for 5 seconds to toggle the manual sleep on/off as before.

releases.title: Battery charging & charge indicator
changes: Actually enable the AXP2101 LiPo charger (it was configured but never switched on), with a precharge step to revive a deeply-discharged cell.
Enable the fuel gauge so the battery percentage reads correctly.
Report charging/USB state — the dashboard now shows a ⚡ charging indicator instead of a bogus 0%.
Reject implausible battery voltage readings so the level no longer flickers to 0%.

releases.title: Sleep schedule (quiet hours)
changes: New per-screen sleep schedule — the panel shows a Sleeping page and stops refreshing between a set start and end time, saving battery.
Wakes are now aligned to the exact minute/hour instead of drifting later each cycle.

releases.title: Battery life & reliability
changes: Keep the panel rails powered across sleep so the display refreshes reliably on every wake (the AXP2101 wouldn't re-init on a timer wake).
Power the WiFi radio down during the long e-ink refresh — a big per-wake battery saving.
Skip the boot-button reconfigure window on unattended wakes; faster, cheaper wake-ups via cached WiFi channel.
A configured device that briefly can't reach WiFi now sleeps and retries instead of getting stuck in setup mode.

releases.title: Refresh interval & update screens
changes: Honour the screen's configured refresh interval as the base, instead of falling back to a fixed 5 minutes.
Show an 'Updating firmware' screen during an update and an 'Update complete' confirmation afterwards.
Removed a redundant panel power-on for a slightly shorter refresh.

releases.title: Reliable wake-up display
changes: Fix the screen staying blank after a deep-sleep wake — the AXP2101 power chip sometimes failed to re-init coming out of sleep, so the panel never got power.
Power chip now fully resets the I2C bus between attempts instead of retrying a stuck bus.
Added a one-shot clean restart that reliably recovers a failed power-up, guarded so it can never boot-loop.

releases.title: Faster refresh & on-screen version
changes: Shorter, per-step display timeouts so refreshes complete sooner.
Device now reports the firmware version it is running, shown on the screen page.
Logs are sent to the server so issues can be diagnosed without a serial cable.

releases.title: E6 panel bring-up & OTA
changes: Correct E6 Spectra-6 init sequence, pin map and panel power via the AXP2101 PMIC.
Fixed upside-down images (180° rotation).
Opt-in over-the-air updates — update from the screen page or enable auto-update per screen, no reflashing.
QR-code pairing flow to link a device to a screen.
